integration_validation_v2.gap: MT-195 requires validation-matrix rows for stale README/spec detection and path portability (reject drive-letter/user-profile paths). No such matrix_kind/rows exist in the diagnostics catalog. Cited mt_195_crash_recovery_scenarios tests WP-KERNEL-004 crash-recovery harness, unrelated to stale-source/path-portability validation.
integration_validation_v2.evidence: tests/session_checkpoint_tests.rs:257 (mt_195_crash_recovery_scenarios — crash recovery, not stale-doc/portability); src/atelier/state_probe.rs:782-792 (no portability/stale matrix kind; MT-167 stale-README detector at :1481 is a separate finding type, not the MT-195 matrix)

integration_validation_v3.evidence: Typed kind STALE_SOURCE_PATH_PORTABILITY at state_probe.rs:815, in ALL :831. Real executable two-layer portability gate: reject_nonportable_path (state_probe.rs:943-961) layered on reject_legacy_runtime_ref (atelier/mod.rs:356, drive-letter rejection :380-386), hard-gated for this kind (:995-998). 4 rows at state_probe.rs:1417-1453. Proofs: tests/atelier_diagnostics_validation_matrix_tests.rs:484 (managed PG + EventLedger) and :516 mt195_nonportable_evidence_refs_are_rejected - 5 nonportable-ref probes asserting the precise rejecting layer, fail-closed against live PG.
integration_validation_v3.basis: v3 adversarial per-MT review of remediated deliverable + runtime proof via full-crate managed-PG sweep and kernel-builder fresh-DB proof logs

minimum_controls: Use contract-first identity and preserve projection hash.
risks: Imported MT contract may under-capture prose-only review or proof instructions.
